  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Troweling bleed water back into the concrete surface, which will cause dusting, scaling, and a weak surface layer."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop finishing operations immediately.",
    "Allow bleed water to evaporate naturally or remove it using a hose, squeegee, or dragging a burlap/rope across the surface.",
    "Do not resume troweling until all bleed water has disappeared and the concrete has started its initial set."
  ],
  "rationale": "Finishing concrete while bleed water is still present on the surface traps water under the trowel, increasing the water-cement ratio at the surface. This leads to a weak, non-durable surface layer prone to dusting, scaling, and delamination.",
  "references": [
    "ACI 302.1R (Guide for Concrete Floor and Slab Construction)",
    "ACI 308R (Guide to External Curing of Concrete)"
  ]
}
